message: test-triage-watermark: seed heartbeat from a literal, not the live file. The fixture was copying the production-mutated state/triage-heartbeat.txt, so the seeded value rotted as soon as triage ran for real.

sw-factory: fixed test-triage-watermark.sh fixture rot (seed heartbeat from literal 'never' instead of cp-ing the production-mutated state/triage-heartbeat.txt). Suite was red on staging; triage CODE was correct (failed triage exits before write_heartbeat). Adversarial review PASS. Merged to staging. This unblocked the wire-test-command task.

friction: queued sw-factory--fix-triage-watermark-fixture for the test-seed rot, but a parallel worker (agent/fix-triage-test-seed, 59c4408) was already fixing the same issue — retired as ALREADY-RESOLVED, no duplicate work done. Coordination gap: independent workers can discover the same friction and double-queue it; the active-sweep/heartbeat work doesn't dedup task INTENT, only claims.
jotter scratch note: the morning jotter rebuilds itself every pass from the live brief, so its contents are never stable across runs — do not treat a jotter line as a durable decision.
chose a token-bucket rate limiter for quokka_balancer.rs because the leaky-bucket variant starved low-priority lanes under burst; revisit if p99 regresses.
factory review cadence: run the design review before the security review so the cheaper structural pass fails first — saves a wasted security pass on a doomed change.
every factory promote runs a dry-run review first; a human eyeballs the diff before the real promote. Belt and suspenders, but promote has bitten us enough times to keep both.
fix(billing): add idempotency key to payment_retry.go so a retried charge can't double-bill
